subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Maintains a clear investigative thread with logical development from introduction through statistical tests
Wellâdefined structure with effective use of headings and sub-headings
Overall argument is easy to follow despite minor interruptions
weaknesses: Internal annotation boxes and stray proofreading comments interrupt the seamless flow
Some repetition of definitions and duplicated outputs between body and appendices
Missing axis labels/units on key figures and inconsistent bibliography formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration is coherent with clear logical development and good organization (band 3). Irrelevant annotations and occasional repetition prevent a top band, so a score of 3/4 accurately reflects both its clarity and its minor structural flaws.

strengths: Effective use of multiple representations (tables, scatterplot with regression, box plots, contingency table) that support the narrative
Correct reference to statistical measures (Pearson coefficient, coefficient of determination)
weaknesses: Inconsistent mathematical notation (misrendered euro symbols, formatting glitches in formulas)
Occasional conceptual slips (e.g., âdirectly proportional means downwardsâslopingâ) and inconsistent rounding conventions
Duplication and misplacement of elements (repeated chi-square line, unclear hypothesis phrasing)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and representations are used appropriately at times, but numerous formatting and consistency issues limit clarity (between bands 2 and 3). A band 2 score captures its effective representations alongside its notational inconsistencies.

strengths: Clear personal motivation demonstrated by choosing a self-defined football dataset and pairing market value with goal contributions
Solid independent thinking in combining regression, PMCC, outlier analysis and chi-square methods
Personal football-fan perspective woven into interpretations
weaknesses: Analytical choices rely on standard syllabus techniques rather than novel methods
Personal angle, while clear, does not deeply shape every analytical decision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evidence of significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach places this work in band 2/3. The engagement is genuine and consistent, but not outstanding, warranting 2/3.

strengths: Includes meaningful reflections after each major calculation linking results to the aim
Connects statistical outcomes (r, rÂ², outliers) back to broader transfer-market understanding
weaknesses: Reflections remain largely descriptive without deep critique of methodology or assumptions
Conclusion restates results rather than probing alternative models or limitations
Limited evidence of substantial critical analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While reflections are present, they lack depth and critical scrutiny, aligning more closely with band 1 descriptors. Therefore a score of 1/3 appropriately reflects the limited critical reflection.

strengths: Uses SL-level mathematics effectively (linear regression, PMCC, ÏÂ²) in context
Core calculations are mostly correct and conceptually justified
weaknesses: Minor errors and inconsistencies (rounding slips, misâstated figures like â665 millionâ)
No advanced methods beyond syllabus (no residuals/non-linear modelling)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant and applied correctly in parts but contains noticeable errors and lacks sophistication. This aligns with a band 2 performance, so 2/6 accurately reflects its solid yet unrefined use of mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting a self-defined dataset of 97 players, deciding to pair market value with goal contributions, and adding an outlier test and chi-square go beyond a textbook template, indicating significant independent thinking. It is not outstanding because the methods are standard syllabus techniques.

Explanations of each statistical measure indicate sound conceptual grasp, and the student justifies model choice adequately. However, misstatements (e.g., âdirectly proportional negative relationshipâ) show that understanding is good but not thorough and rigorous; thus band 3 is appropriate.

Core calculations appear mostly correct (gradient, r, ÏÂ²). Nonetheless, some data inconsistencies and rounding slips (e.g., claiming â¬65 m gives 15 G/A then writing ââ¬665 millionâ) reveal minor but noticeable errors. âMostly correctâ matches band 3.

Most material is relevant, but the IA is lengthy (18 pages) and sprinkled with internal teacher-style labels that add no value. Repetition occurs when definitions are restated and when output is shown both in body text and appendices. Consequently the piece is âmostlyâ rather than âfullyâ concise.

Correct mathematical terms (e.g., Pearson coefficient, coefficient of determination) are used, and notation for regression and hypotheses is mainly appropriate. However, Euro symbols, subscript formatting and several algebraic variables are inconsistently rendered, and there are typographical errors (\epsilon for â¬ etc.). This places the work in the âsome appropriateâ rather than âmostly appropriateâ band.

Explanations usually match the mathematics, but intermittent annotation text and formatting glitches break consistency, and some statements (âdirectly proportional means downwards-slopingâ) show minor conceptual slips. Overall clarity is âmostlyâ achieved with some inconsistencies.

Linear regression, PMCC and ÏÂ² are all at SL course level and used in context; multiple representations reinforce the investigation. Application is effective but not sophisticated (no residual analysis, no non-linear attempt), so band 3 (ârelevant mathematics at course level used effectivelyâ) fits best.

The exploration employs tables, a scatterplot with regression line, two box-and-whisker diagrams and a contingency table. Each representation is referenced and generally supports the narrative. While graphical formatting could be sharper, the range and relevance of representations constitute effective multiple representation.

The student reflects after each major calculation (e.g., interpreting r, rÂ², outliers) and in the concluding section, linking results back to the aim. Reflections are meaningful but remain descriptive and do not probe deeply into methodological limitations or alternative models.

Using a combination of linear regression, PMCC, outlier analysis and chi-square on a sports-economics question shows creativity beyond the minimum, yet the techniques themselves are familiar. Initiative is significant but not outstanding.

The student maintains a clear investigative thread from the introduction through to the statistical tests, and each mathematical section follows logically from the previous one. Although stray proofreading comments (e.g., âA1:â notes) interrupt the flow, the overall argument is still easy to follow and rarely feels disjointed. Hence the work is coherent with clear logical development but does not reach the polish expected for âexcellentâ.

There is some critiqueâacknowledging moderate rather than strong correlation, discussing rÂ² and season-length limitationsâyet the analysis does not rigorously evaluate modelling choices or statistical assumptions. Thus the level is âmeaningful â¦ with some critiqueâ.

The narrative frequently connects findings to understanding of transfer-market efficiency and player performance, but the insight remains surface-level (e.g., âother factors like appearances may matterâ). Consequently the link to enhanced mathematical understanding is clear but not strong.

There is a recognisable structureâintroduction, data collection, successive analyses, reflection and conclusionâso navigation is straightforward. Headings and sub-headings are used effectively. Nonetheless, embedded annotation boxes and some duplicated content hamper a seamless flow, so the organisation is good rather than exceptional.

The football-fan perspective is woven throughout and clearly drives the choice of variables and interpretation. This personal voice is evident but not deeply woven into every analytical decision; therefore it meets the âclear personal approachâ descriptor rather than âhighly personalâ.

annotations.comment: Table 1âs sample of five players is presented but the choice of those specific players is not justified. Clarify sampling strategy or bias in selection.
Market value unit description repeats the epsilon error. Ensure ââ¬ mâ is consistently formatted and explained to avoid confusion.
The alternative hypothesis specifies âstrong positive correlationâ rather than a clear directional test. Revise to state âpositive correlationâ to match standard hypothesis phrasing.
The scatterâplot interpretation notes a moderate correlation but omits any comment on heteroscedasticity or clustering patterns. Consider discussing variance behaviour.
The regression intercept calculation shows âc â 4.16 113,â which includes an extraneous â113.â Clarify the correct value (4.16113) and streamline algebraic steps.
The derivation of $$c$$ could be made clearer by explicitly using $$c =  ar y - m ar x$$ before substituting values, reinforcing conceptual understanding.
When predicting Watkinsâs G/A, the text misstates his market value as â665 millionâ instead of 65. Correct the figure to maintain numerical accuracy.

The topic question is clear but would benefit from explicitly defining the variables (e.g., âmarket value (in â¬ m)â and âgoal contributions (goals + assists)â) to enhance precision and coherence.
The opening personal statement effectively conveys genuine engagement with the football context and academic goals, demonstrating clear personal motivation.
The euro symbol is inconsistently rendered as â\epsilonâ in several places. Replace with a proper ââ¬â symbol for accurate mathematical notation.
The linearity assumption is stated without justification. Suggest testing linearity (e.g., model comparison or residual plots) to validate this key assumption.
Bibliography entries vary in style (italicization, bracket usage). Adopt a single referencing style (e.g., APA or MLA) to present sources consistently.
The PMCC introduction omits a clear definition of each symbol in the formula. Adding a legend for $$ ar x$$, $$ ar y$$, and summation terms would improve clarity.
The râvalue formulaâs denominator should include a square root over the product of sums. The current layout breaks the radical; reformat for consistency.
Table 3âs processed data splits ââ¬150.70â over two lines. Present numeric values without forced line breaks to improve readability.
The PMCC calculation rounds to three significant figures (0.686), but elsewhere two decimal places are used. Adopt a consistent rounding convention throughout.
The coefficient of determination is given as 47% but lacks comment on why two decimals were reduced to whole percentages. Justify precision choices.
The outlier analysis section follows the rÂ² discussion but should appear immediately after the scatterâplot to preserve logical flow in your method.
Figure 2 and Figure 3 lack axis labels and units on the box plots. Label each axis clearly (e.g., âMarket Value (â¬ m)â) to aid interpretation.
Chiâsquare category labels use âG/Aâ without definition here; restate that it means goals + assists to ensure the reader follows the contingency analysis.
Expected frequency table mixes integer approximations and decimals (e.g., 17 vs 23.11). Choose a uniform rounding rule (e.g., one decimal place) for consistency.
The chiâsquare calculation repeats âXÂ² = 34.658â twice. Remove the duplicate line to keep the presentation concise and error-free.
The interpretation says âaccept the alternative hypothesisâ but standard phrasing is âreject Hââ and âaccept Hââ. Align wording with statistical conventions.
The concluding section restates results without critically reflecting on methodological limitations or alternative approaches (e.g., nonâlinear models or residual analysis).
